rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section to support a key premise: when private-self processes are salient, consumers choose bolder and more unconventional options. That premise directly feeds the citing paper's main hypothesis that smartphone use increases private self-focus and therefore uniqueness seeking. The citation is not just a background list item; it provides specific conceptual support for the mechanism and interpretation of results, even though the authors do not directly reuse the cited paper's methods.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Maimaran and Simonson (2011) as prior evidence linking self-expression/private-self orientation to unconventional choice. It uses that logic as conceptual groundwork for predicting and explaining smartphone-driven increases in preference for unique options.
